locator: Results, 'Variation Across Samples and Settings' subsection, p. 149
summary: In the Variation section the authors report that effect-size heterogeneity concentrated among the very large effects, explicitly naming anchoring first. Table 2 corroborates this: all four anchoring tasks (babies born, Mt. Everest, Chicago, distance to NYC) replicated with median d approximately 1.17-2.42, all p < .001, and a credibility of 1.00.
short_quote: heterogeneity of effect sizes was largely observed among the very large effects – anchoring, allowed-forbidden, and relations between implicit and explicit attitudes
